questions.q: If sin x = 3/5, then find the value of cos x.
Prove that 2sin^(2) x + sin x = 0 has no solution for 0° ≤ x ≤ 360°.
questions.answer: Let 2sin^(2) x + sin x = 0, we get 2sin^(2) x = -sin x, which is sin x(2sin x + 1) = 0.

questions.q: If tan x = 1/2 and tan x lies in the second quadrant, find the value of sin x.
If sin 2x = 3sin x cos x, show that tan x = 3.
questions.answer: We have sin 2x = 3sin x cos x, which is 2sin x cos x = 3sin x cos x. This implies cos x = 3/2.

questions.q: If cos x = 7/25, find the value of tan x.
Prove that (sin x + cos x)^(2) + (sin x - cos x)^(2) = 2 + 2sin^(2) x.
questions.answer: LHS = (sin x + cos x)^(2) + (sin x - cos x)^(2) = (sin^(2) x + 2sin x cos x + cos^(2) x) + (sin^(2) x - 2sin x cos x + cos^(2) x) = 2sin^(2) x + 2cos^(2) x = 2 + 2sin^(2) x.

questions.q: If tan x = 3/4, find the value of sin x.
Prove that 2cos^(2) x - sin x - 1 = 0 has no solution for 0° ≤ x ≤ 360°.
questions.answer: Let 2cos^(2) x - sin x - 1 = 0, we get 2cos^(2) x = sin x + 1, which is 2(1 - sin^(2) x) = sin x + 1.

questions.q: If cos x = 12/13, find the value of tan x.
Prove that (sin x + cos x)^(2) + (sin x - cos x)^(2) = 2 + 2cos^(2) x.
questions.answer: LHS = (sin x + cos x)^(2) + (sin x - cos x)^(2) = (sin^(2) x + 2sin x cos x + cos^(2) x) + (sin^(2) x - 2sin x cos x + cos^(2) x) = 2sin^(2) x + 2cos^(2) x = 2 + 2cos^(2) x.
